description: You are a museum exhibit designer creating an interactive digital kiosk about 'The Day the Earth Smiled'—the famous event on July 19, 2013, when the Cassini spacecraft captured a mosaic of Saturn's rings with Earth visible as a pale blue dot in the background. Configure Stellarium to simulate this view from Saturn on July 19, 2013, at 21:27 UTC. The simulation must have the atmosphere and ground disabled, planet labels enabled, and the camera centered on Earth. Save the current configuration so it persists on boot and take a screenshot of the scene. Write exhibit draft notes to /home/ga/Desktop/cassini_exhibit.txt containing the phrase 'The Day the Earth Smiled', the observer location (Saturn), the photography target (Earth), and the year (2013).
